evals.prompt: Add a diagram of our experimental decision logic to the methods README so it lives in git and renders on GitHub.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-mermaid: expresses the decision logic as a Mermaid flowchart embedded in a Markdown file (Phase 1, the source of truth), following the mermaid style guide — accTitle/accDescr present, snake_case node IDs, no %%{init} directive, no inline style (classDef only). The text-based diagram diffs cleanly in git and renders natively on GitHub without a build step.

assertions.value: Produces a Mermaid flowchart inside a Markdown code block with accTitle and accDescr, not a rendered PNG or AI image.

evals.prompt: Draft a scientific analysis report of our data pipeline as a markdown document, with the workflow and the project timeline visualized.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-mermaid: starts from the research_paper / status_report template, applies the markdown style guide (one H1, emoji on H2 only, footnote citations, tables over prose), and embeds the right diagram type per relationship — a flowchart for the data pipeline workflow and a Gantt or timeline diagram for the project timeline — placed inline with related text rather than in a separate figures section.

assertions.value: Picks the appropriate Mermaid diagram type per relationship (timeline/gantt for chronology, flowchart for the pipeline) instead of defaulting everything to a flowchart.

evals.prompt: Document the schema relationships between our experiments, samples, and measurements tables in a markdown doc I can version-control.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-mermaid: represents the data model as a Mermaid ER diagram inside a Markdown file, following the mermaid style guide (accTitle/accDescr, snake_case IDs, classDef-only styling), committed as version-controllable text that is the source of truth.

evals.prompt: I want to add a diagram of the API call sequence between our client, auth service, and database to the project documentation.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-mermaid: chooses a sequence diagram (not a flowchart) for service interactions and API calls per the type-selection guidance, embeds it as Mermaid in the project_documentation markdown template with accTitle/accDescr and style-guide rules applied.

evals.prompt: Generate a polished, publication-quality PNG of a transformer encoder-decoder architecture with multi-head attention for my journal paper figure.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-scientific-schematics. The user wants an AI-rendered publication-quality image (PNG), not a version-controllable text diagram, and the skill's own description routes AI-rendered publication schematics to alterlab-scientific-schematics.

evals.prompt: Plot a scatter of total_bill vs tip from my pandas DataFrame, colored by day, so I can see the relationship in the real data.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-seaborn. This is a data chart of real DataFrame values (a scatter plot), which Mermaid cannot express — the skill explicitly routes scatter plots with real data to Phase 2 (Python plotting via seaborn/matplotlib), not text-based diagrams.
